scheme_candidates.reaction_name: Ring-Closing Alkyne Metathesis in Azamacrolide Synthesis

reaction_details.notes: The reaction proceeds in multiple steps. First, a ring-closing alkyne metathesis (RCAM) is performed using either a tungsten or molybdenum catalyst. The resulting cycloalkyne is then reduced with Lindlar's catalyst and deprotected with TBAF to yield the final product. Yields for the RCAM step are 71% (W cat.) or 67% (Mo cat.). The hydrogenation yield is 94% and the deprotection yield is 62%.

scheme_candidates.reaction_name: Ring-Closing Alkyne Metathesis in Prostaglandin E2-1,15-lactone Synthesis

reaction_details.notes: A three-step synthesis involving RCAM (68-73% yield), Lindlar reduction (86% yield), and TBS deprotection (88% yield).

scheme_candidates.reaction_name: Ring-Closing Alkyne Metathesis in Turriane Synthesis

reaction_details.notes: The RCAM step to form the cyclophane proceeds in 83% yield. The final product, Turriane, is obtained after subsequent deprotection steps.

scheme_candidates.reaction_summary: Intramolecular cyclization of amino ketols via an Amadori reaction to synthesize piperidin-3-one derivatives.
Intramolecular Amadori rearrangement of monosaccharide esters of leucine-enkephaline to afford bicyclic ketoses.

products.name: Bicyclic ketoses (I: R1=H, R2=OH; II: R1=OH, R2=H)

Diastereoselective zwitterionic aza-Claisen rearrangement in the synthesis of (+)-dihydrocanadensolide

products.name: N-alkyl-C-allyl glycine ester
products.selectivity_comment: anti:syn. Yields correspond to R=H (51%) and R=Me (63%).

scheme_candidates.notes: The reaction is a multi-step sequence where the Baeyer-Villiger oxidation is a key step.
The reaction is a multi-step sequence where the Baeyer-Villiger oxidation is a key step.
Multi-step synthesis of cage-annulated ethers. Stepwise yields are provided: 93% (BV), 73% (reduction), 80% (cyclization).
Alternative multi-step synthesis of cage-annulated ethers. Stepwise yields are provided: 20% (BV), 80% (reduction), 60% (cyclization).
Multi-step synthesis involving a Baeyer-Villiger oxidation followed by a trifluoroacetic acid-catalyzed cleavage and rearrangement. The yield for the first step (BV oxidation) is 80%.

scheme_candidates.description: Overall scheme for the Balz-Schiemann reaction, where an aryl amine is converted to an aryl fluoride via an aryl diazonium tetrafluoroborate intermediate.
Mechanism for the formation of the aryldiazonium salt from an aryl amine and nitrosonium ion, which is generated from nitrous acid.
Mechanism for the decomposition of the aryldiazonium salt to an aryl fluoride, involving an aryl cation intermediate.
A synthetic application of the Balz-Schiemann reaction to incorporate a fluorine atom into an estrone derivative.

scheme_candidates.scheme_text: Overall reaction for the Barton Radical Decarboxylation, converting a carboxylic acid to an alkane.
Initiation step of the Barton Radical Decarboxylation mechanism, showing the formation of the tri-n-butyltin radical.
Propagation step of the Barton Radical Decarboxylation mechanism.
Synthetic application of the Barton decarboxylation procedure in the total synthesis of (-)-verrucarol.
1,3-bishomocubane carboxylic acid is converted to X=Br (87%) or X=SePh (94%) using CCl3Br or (PhSe)2 under Barton Reaction conditions.
A bis-thiohydroxamic ester is irradiated in the presence of citraconic anhydride to form Tyromycin A in 79% yield.
scheme_candidates.reaction_string: bis-thiohydroxamic ester + citraconic anhydride -> Tyromycin A

scheme_candidates.description: General scheme for the Barton-McCombie radical deoxygenation reaction.
Initiation step of the Barton-McCombie reaction mechanism.
Propagation step of the Barton-McCombie reaction mechanism.
Application of the Barton-McCombie deoxygenation in the asymmetric synthesis of the C1-C19 fragment of kabiramide C.
Stepwise hydroboration of an alkene with borane to form a trialkylborane.
Mechanism for the concerted addition of borane to a double bond via a four-centered transition state.
Catalytic cycle for the Cp2Ti-catalyzed hydroboration of alkenes.

annotations.text: The product shown, 2-amino-5-phenylpyridine, is an isomer of the expected product from 3-phenylpyridine, which would be 2-amino-3-phenylpyridine or 6-amino-3-phenylpyridine.

This is the final intramolecular Chichibabin cyclization step of a multi-step synthesis. The yield of 94% is for the last two steps of the sequence (deprotection and cyclization). The reactant structure is inferred from the overall transformation.

scheme_candidates.reaction_name: Lewis Acid Mediated Stereoselective Claisen Rearrangement
Double Claisen Rearrangement/Intramolecular Diels-Alder Reaction Cascade

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: An aldehyde or ketone is reduced to an alkane using zinc amalgam (Zn(Hg)) and hydrochloric acid (HCl) or another hydrogen halide (HX) in an organic solvent.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: In 1913, E. Clemmensen reported that simple ketones and aldehydes were converted to the corresponding alkanes upon refluxing for several hours with 40% aqueous hydrochloric acid, amalgamated zinc (Zn/Hg), and a hydrophobic organic co-solvent such as toluene. This method of converting a carbonyl group to the corresponding methylene group is known as the Clemmensen reduction.

extracts.transformation_text: Reduction of an aldehyde or ketone to an alkane.
extracts.conditions_text: 40% HCl (aq.) or dry HX; X = Cl or Br

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: A multi-step carbanionic mechanism for the Clemmensen reduction is shown, starting from a ketone reacting with zinc and chloride, proceeding through protonation, dehydration, further reaction with zinc to form a carbanion, and final protonation to yield the alkane.

extracts.transformation_text: Carbanionic mechanism for the Clemmensen reduction.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: A multi-step carbenoid mechanism for the Clemmensen reduction is depicted, starting with a ketone reacting with zinc, followed by further reaction with zinc, protonation, dehydration to form a zinc carbenoid intermediate, and final protonation to yield the alkane.

extracts.transformation_text: Carbenoid mechanism for the Clemmensen reduction.
